name: Moncalvillo BTT Center Loop
description: This difficult 26.8-mile mountain biking trail near Navarrete offers challenging terrain, vineyard views, and traditional guardaviñas.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Navarrete, located in the Ebro Valley of La Rioja, Spain, offers diverse terrain for cyclists. The region spans from the Ebro depression to the Moncalvillo Mountains, providing varied elevations and landscapes. Trails wind through picturesque vineyards and dense forests of pines, beeches, and oaks. Notable natural features include the Valbornedo Reservoir and Tedeón Hill, which offer scenic backdrops for rides. Best mountain bike trails around Navarrete The most popular mountain bike trail is Moncalvillo BTT Center Loop, a 26.8 miles (43.2 km) difficult route that takes 4 hours 14 minutes to complete. This challenging trail features significant elevation gain through varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Fountain in Navarrete – Embalse de Valbornedo loop from Navarrete, a moderate 12.9 miles (20.7 km) path. This route offers a balanced ride with moderate elevation changes and scenic views around the Valbornedo Reservoir. Local mountain bikers also love the Fountain in Navarrete loop from Navarrete, a 8.7 miles (14.0 km) trail leading through local terrain, often completed in about 51 minutes. Mountain biking around Navarrete is defined by rolling vineyards, dense forests, and the varied topography from the Ebro depression to the Moncalvillo Mountains. The extensive network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from easy family rides to challenging ascents. The routes in Navarrete are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 300 reviews. More than 4000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Navarrete's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Navarrete?
A: Navarrete is a hub for mountain biking, offering access to an extensive network of routes. The Moncalvillo BTT Center, which Navarrete is a reception point for, boasts over 250 kilometers of mountain biking routes across ten municipalities. On komoot, you can find 17 distinct mountain bike tours in the immediate vicinity of Navarrete, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Navarrete?
A: The terrain around Navarrete is incredibly diverse, ranging from the lower Ebro depression to the peaks of the Sierra del Moncalvillo mountains. You'll find trails weaving through tranquil vineyard areas, dense forests of pines, beeches, and oaks, and paths around natural features like the Valbornedo Reservoir. This variety offers everything from gentle paths to more demanding ascents and descents.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Navarrete?
A: Yes, Navarrete offers several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or family rides. The Moncalvillo BTT Center includes 'green' routes designed for less experienced riders. On komoot, you can find 5 easy routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17545616" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain in Navarrete loop from Navarrete&lt;/a&gt;, which is 14 km long with minimal elevation gain.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Navarrete?
A: Absolutely. For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, Navarrete and the Moncalvillo BTT Center offer 'black' and 'red' routes with vibrant descents and significant elevation changes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993655187/moncalvillo-btt-center-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncalvillo BTT Center Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 43.2 km route with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38250973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Antón Hill – Valbornedo Reservoir loop from Navarrete&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 57.1 km ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Navarrete?
A: The mountain bike trails around Navarrete are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 4,000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic beauty of the vineyards and forests, as well as the diverse range of routes available for all skill levels.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer scenic views or pass by natural features?
A: Many trails around Navarrete offer picturesque views and pass by notable natural features. You can ride around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/682737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the Valbornedo Reservoir, as seen on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10464179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain in Navarrete – Embalse de Valbornedo loop from Navarrete&lt;/a&gt;. The town itself is built on Tedeón Hill, which provides panoramic vistas of the surrounding area, including towns and mountains, often incorporated into routes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Navarrete?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Navarrete are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Popular examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993655187/moncalvillo-btt-center-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moncalvillo BTT Center Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10464179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain in Navarrete – Embalse de Valbornedo loop from Navarrete&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10464512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valbornedo Reservoir – Fountain in Navarrete loop from Navarrete&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly mountain bike trails in Navarrete?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on mountain bike trails can vary, many natural areas in La Rioja are generally accessible for dogs, especially on leash. It's always best to check local signage or the specific trail's guidelines before heading out. The extensive network of paths, including those on the Camino de Santiago, often allows for dog walking alongside cycling.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Navarrete?
A: Navarrete, as a reception point for the Moncalvillo BTT Center, is equipped to welcome cyclists. You can typically find parking within the town itself, especially near the BTT Center facilities. For specific trailheads, local parking options are usually available, though it's advisable to check maps or local information for the most convenient spots.

Q: Do I need any permits for mountain biking in the Moncalvillo BTT Center area?
A: The Moncalvillo BTT Center routes are certified by the International Mountain Bicycling Association (IMBA) and are generally open for public use. While specific permits are not typically required for general access to these trails, it's always a good idea to check the official Moncalvillo BTT Center website or local tourist information for any updated regulations or special event requirements.

Q: Can I rent a mountain bike in Navarrete?
A: Given Navarrete's status as a mountain biking hub and a reception point for the Moncalvillo BTT Center, it is highly likely that bike rental services are available in or very close to the town. These services often cater to various types of bikes, including mountain bikes, to support the numerous visitors exploring the region's trails.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Navarrete?
A: La Rioja generally enjoys a favorable climate for outdoor activities. Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are often considered the best times for mountain biking in Navarrete. During these seasons, temperatures are mild, and the landscapes are particularly beautiful, with vineyards either budding or displaying their autumnal colors. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, while winters can be cooler and wetter.
tips: The guardaviñas, huts or huts, are rural stone buildings, typical of La Rioja. Most of those that remain today were built in the 19th century. They were used as a shelter for vine farmers and their work animals from the inclement weather. They were also used by Field Guard institutions to monitor the crops from these locations.
Guardaviñas or hut, in perfect condition, at the crossroads of the access track to the town of Sorzano.

The highest point of the refrigerator route, where you can enjoy beautiful views. The antenna and the snow house are private, but if you get caught in the rain, you can take refuge in a hut in the area of the snowfields.
Drinking water fountain at the foot of the road to Navarrete. Opposite is the cemetery whose door is that of the old pilgrims' hospital. The road to Santiago passes through there.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Navarrete, Moncalvillo – circular pasando guardaviñas trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Moncalvillo Commonwealth, which hosts the Centro BTT Moncalvillo. While specific parking areas for this exact route aren't detailed, the Moncalvillo MTB Center is a good starting point for routes in the area, suggesting designated parking facilities are available there or near trailheads in towns like Navarrete.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is part of the Centro BTT Moncalvillo, the first off-road bicycle center in La Rioja. Generally, access to official MTB centers in Spain is free, but it's always a good idea to check the Centro BTT Moncalvillo's official website for any specific regulations or potential fees, especially for organized events or services. No permits are typically required for individual rides.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is categorized as difficult and demands very good fitness and advanced riding skills. It corresponds to STS S3-S6, indicating challenging singletrack with obstacles and very difficult terrain that may require advanced bike handling or even pushing your bike in some sections. Beginners should look for easier routes in the Moncalvillo area.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse and beautiful landscapes characteristic of the Moncalvillo region. The route passes through areas surrounded by vineyards, which are typical of La Rioja, as well as lush pine and fir forests and green meadows. Higher points, such as the Alto de Moncalvillo, offer beautiful panoramic views.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail's name, "pasando guardaviñas," suggests you'll pass by traditional vineyard guard huts or structures, such as the Guardaviñas on the Camino de la Mora and the Guardaviñas of Sorzano, which connect to the region's wine heritage. You'll also encounter the Alto de Moncalvillo and the historic Gate of the Old Pilgrims' Hospital of Navarrete.

faqs.question: Is the Navarrete, Moncalvillo – circular pasando guardaviñas trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact trail are not detailed, generally, dogs are allowed on trails in natural areas in Spain, often requiring them to be on a leash. Given the challenging nature of this mountain biking route, ensure your dog is fit enough for the distance and terrain, and always practice responsible pet ownership by cleaning up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The Moncalvillo region offers pleasant riding conditions during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, especially in La Rioja, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable. Winter rides are possible but be prepared for colder temperatures and potentially wet or muddy conditions, especially on unpaved sections and higher elevations.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the Moncalvillo area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll ride along parts of the Camino de la Mora, Neveras de Moncalvillo, Ruta Dos Dehesas, and the Camino de Santiago. Other smaller segments include Ruta de las Bodegas and various local paths like Cno de Sorzano a Moncalvillo.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and challenging, corresponding to STS S3-S6. You can expect a mix of mountain tracks, singletrack, and potentially some paved sections, though a substantial portion will be unpaved. Be prepared for steep inclines, with some segments reaching almost 13% gradient, and technical sections that demand advanced bike handling skills.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the "Navarrete, Moncalvillo – circular pasando guardaviñas" is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same general area without requiring an out-and-back return on the same path.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a ride on this difficult trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length (approximately 43 km), you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), a first-aid kit, and appropriate mountain biking gear including a helmet, gloves, and protective eyewear. A GPS device or a fully charged phone with the Komoot app is highly recommended for navigation.
